name: agent-loop-designer
description: Turn `$agent-loop-designer <thing I keep doing>` into a reusable Codex loop with same-project nested worktree fan-out by default.

interface.shortDescription: Make recurring Codex work repeatable from one simple command.
interface.longDescription: Agent Loop Designer lets you type `$agent-loop-designer <describe the thing I keep doing>` and turns that recurring work into an executable loop. Independent candidates and code-editing work inside that loop can use nested manual worktrees under the main checkout, with worker threads targeting the same saved Codex project and the current thread acting as coordinator until every worker is implemented, rejected, or blocked. This behavior is scoped to the loop turn and does not change later subagent requests into thread creation. It includes `$architecture-cascade` for running improve-codebase-architecture once, creating candidate worktree-backed threads for that cascade turn, monitoring them, and acting on safe candidates automatically in isolated worktrees. Candidate worktrees stay under `.codex-worktrees/` inside the existing checkout so they do not appear as separate Codex GUI projects or trigger separate project-trust boundaries. Durable loops include a failure-learning rule so repeat failures caused by this plugin feed back into skill or reference updates.
